title: Arrays and Loops
description: Master array manipulation and iteration in Java by finding the maximum value in an integer array. This fundamental skill is essential for test data processing and result validation in test automation.
instructions: Create a method `findMax()` that accepts an int array as parameter
Use a for loop or enhanced for loop to iterate through the array
Track the maximum value found during iteration
Handle edge case: empty array should throw IllegalArgumentException
learningObjectives: Understand Java array syntax and initialization
Master different loop types (for, enhanced for)
Understand primitive data types in arrays

hints.content: Start by initializing a variable 'max' with the first element of the array. Then compare each subsequent element with this max value.
Use a for loop: for (int i = 1; i < numbers.length; i++). Start from index 1 since you already used index 0 for initialization. Update max if numbers[i] > max.
Complete approach: First check if array is empty (throw exception), initialize max = numbers[0], loop through remaining elements updating max when a larger value is found, then return max.
